claimText: India holds the status of an Associate Member at CERN, an international scientific organization located on the Franco-Swiss border.
evidenceExcerpt: India is an Associate Member of CERN, an international scientific organization located on the Franco-Swiss border.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's membership status in international scientific organizations.
distractorHooks: India is a founding member of CERN
India is a full member of CERN
CERN is located exclusively in Switzerland

claimText: Major achievements of CERN include the confirmation of the Higgs boson (God particle) and the development of the World Wide Web.
evidenceExcerpt: CERN's LHC confirmed the existence of the Higgs boson (God particle) and developed the World Wide Web.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on scientific discoveries and their associated institutions.
distractorHooks: The World Wide Web was developed by NASA
The Higgs boson was discovered by Fermilab

claimText: The Large Hadron Collider (LHC) utilizes superconducting magnets to accelerate two high-energy particle beams to speeds approaching the speed of light prior to collision.
evidenceExcerpt: The LHC uses superconducting magnets to accelerate two high-energy particle beams close to the speed of light before collision.
examUse: Statement-based question on the working mechanism of the Large Hadron Collider.
distractorHooks: The LHC uses conventional electromagnets to accelerate particles
The LHC accelerates particles faster than the speed of light

claimText: ATLAS, CMS, ALICE, and LHCb are the four major experimental collaborations operating at CERN's Large Hadron Collider (LHC).
evidenceExcerpt: Four experimental collaborations at CERN's LHC include ATLAS, CMS, ALICE, and LHCb.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching acronyms to their respective space or physics facilities.
distractorHooks: ATLAS and ALICE are NASA space telescopes
CMS is a nuclear reactor in India

claimText: Matter-Antimatter Asymmetry refers to the phenomenon where the observable universe consists almost entirely of matter, even though the Big Bang theoretically produced equal amounts of matter and antimatter.
evidenceExcerpt: Matter-Antimatter Asymmetry refers to the observable universe consisting almost entirely of matter, despite the Big Bang producing equal amounts of both.
examUse: Conceptual statement in a question about cosmology and particle physics.
distractorHooks: The Big Bang produced significantly more matter than antimatter initially
The observable universe consists equally of matter and antimatter

claimText: CP symmetry dictates that the laws of physics remain unchanged if particles are swapped with their corresponding antiparticles and viewed in a mirror.
evidenceExcerpt: CP symmetry implies physics laws remain unchanged if particles are swapped with antiparticles and viewed in a mirror.
examUse: Definition-based question on fundamental symmetries in particle physics.
distractorHooks: CP symmetry implies physics laws change when particles are swapped with antiparticles
CP symmetry only applies to macroscopic objects
